title: Add a fill layer
code: library(toro) library(sf) #> Warning: package 'sf' was built under R version 4.5.2 #> Linking to GEOS 3.13.1, GDAL 3.11.0, PROJ 9.6.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE  base_map <- map() |>   add_feature_server_source(     "https://services8.arcgis.com/AYGZtmUtpARUKBlB/arcgis/rest/services/Te_Reo_M%C4%81ori_Place_Names/FeatureServer/3/query?where=1=1&outFields=*&f=geojson",     "maori_moana_data",     append_query_url = FALSE   ) |>   add_feature_server_source(     "https://services8.arcgis.com/AYGZtmUtpARUKBlB/arcgis/rest/services/Te_Reo_M%C4%81ori_Place_Names/FeatureServer/1/query?where=1=1&outFields=*&f=geojson",     "maori_region_data",     append_query_url = FALSE   ) base_map |>   add_fill_layer(     id = "moana_polygons",     source = "maori_moana_data",     hover_column = "name_mi",     popup_column = "name"   ) base_map |>   add_fill_layer(     id = "region_polygons",     source = "maori_region_data",     hover_column = "name_mi",     popup_column = "name",     paint = get_paint_options(       "fill",       options = list(         colour = "#a3b18a",         opacity = 0.3,         outline_colour = "#588157"       )     )   ) |>   add_fill_layer(     id = "moana_polygons",     source = "maori_moana_data",     hover_column = "name_mi",     popup_column = "name",     paint = get_paint_options(       "fill",       options = list(         colour = get_column_group(           "type",           c(             "lake" = "#a9d6e5",             "reservoir" = "#89c2d9",             "river" = "#61a5c2",             "bay" = "#468faf",             "sea" = "#2c7da0",             "ocean" = "#2a6f97",             "glacier" = "#014f86",             "swamp" = "#01497c",             "lagoon" = "#013a63"           ),           "#012a4a"         )       )     )   )

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: Note: Clusters currently work symbol(icon/pin) layers. Clusters disabled default, symbols coordinates always produce cluster (even can_cluster set false). Clicking cluster one two things: Zoom clusters pins Spiderfy pins cluster become visible
code: base_map  |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes",     source = quakes_data,     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup"   )

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
code: base_map  |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes",     source = quakes_data,     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = TRUE   )

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: can customise cluster appearance using clusterOptions. can set globally creating map specific layers adding layer.

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: clusterOptions.circleOptions changes apperance clusters circle. Available circle (paint/layout) properties can found .
code: map(   imageSources = list(     list(       id = "map_pin",       url = "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/f/f2/678111-map-marker-512.png"     )   ),   # Setting the appearance globally for all clusters in the map   clusterOptions = list(     circleOptions = list(       paint = list(         "circle-color" = "#ebc642",         "circle-stroke-color" = "#fc038c",         "circle-radius" = 50       )     )   ) ) |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes",     source = quakes_data |> head(nrow(quakes_data) / 2),     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = TRUE   ) |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes_two",     source = quakes_data |> tail(nrow(quakes_data) / 2),     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = TRUE,     # Override the global cluster appearance for this layer only     clusterOptions = list(       circleOptions = list(         paint = list(           "circle-color" = "green",           "circle-stroke-color" = "orange",           "circle-radius" = 20         )       )     )   )

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: clusterOptions.textOptions changes apperance clusters text. Available text(symbol) (paint/layout) properties can found .
code: base_map  |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes",     source = quakes_data,     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = TRUE,     clusterOptions = list(       textOptions = list(         paint = list(           "text-color" = "red"         )       )     )   )

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: example advanced customising (see example).
code: base_map |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes",     source = quakes_data,     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = TRUE,     clusterOptions = list(       circleOptions = list(         paint = list(           "circle-color" = get_column_step_steps(             "point_count",             c(2, 5, 10),             c("red", "orange", "yellow", "green")           ),           "circle-stroke-color" = get_column_step_steps(             "point_count",             c(2, 5, 10),             c("red", "orange", "yellow", "green")           ),           "circle-radius" = get_column_step_steps(             "point_count",             c(2, 5, 10),             c(10, 20, 30, 40)           )         ),         layout = list(           "circle-sort-key" = get_column_step_steps(             "point_count",             c(2, 5, 10),             c(4, 3, 2, 1)           )         )       )     )   )

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: Spiderfying handles viewing multiple pins cluster (pins close proximity). map zoomed max zoom level cluster still appear spiderfy clicked . user able view pins even appear cluster. two reasons cluster need spiderfy: maps max zoom still pins close enough coordinates cluster (layer clustering enabled) group pins share coordinates. case, regardless whether layer clustering enabled cluster always appear. due fact without way user know multiple pins stacked top look like single pin.

what: Customise max pins shown in spiderfy
title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: default spiderfying shows 100 pins. can see example one clusters says 501 points can see 100 pins spiderfying. can changed setting clusterOptions.spiderfyOptions.maxSpiderfyPins creating map. , want override global setting specific layer, can set clusterOptions.spiderfyOptions.maxSpiderfyPins adding layer.
code: map(   imageSources = list(     list(       id = "map_pin",       url = "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/f/f2/678111-map-marker-512.png"     )   ),   clusterOptions = list(     spiderfyOptions = list(       maxSpiderfyPins = 500     )   ) ) |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "duplicated_pins",     source = duplicated_data,     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = FALSE,     clusterOptions = list(       spiderfyOptions = list(         # maxSpiderfyPins = 5 # This would override the global setting for this layer only       )     )   )

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: Like clusters can customise spiderfys appearance changing clusterOptions.spiderfyOptions.maxSpiderfyPins. Spiderfying appearance can set globally, per layer. Available line (paint/layout) properties can found .
code: map(   imageSources = list(     list(       id = "map_pin",       url = "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/f/f2/678111-map-marker-512.png"     )   ),   clusterOptions = list(     spiderfyOptions = list(       paint = list(         "line-color" = "purple",         "line-width" = 2       )     )   ) ) |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "duplicated_pins",     source = duplicated_data,     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = FALSE   )

title: map-tiles
text: can find tile options using get_tile_options(). [1] “natgeo” “satellite” “topo” “terrain” “streets” “shaded” [7] “lightgrey” declaring map, can specify style tiles want map start using style parameter. default, style set lightgrey, can change options available. want ability map change tiles need set loadedTiles parameter vector tiles want able switch . can also supply options tile set. Note: loadedTiles either needs character vector (like ) names list (like ). two combined. want supply options certain tile set just make empty list. Note: Currently option supported maxZoom added future.
code: get_tile_options() map(style="satellite") map(   style="satellite",   loadedTiles = c("satellite", "lightgrey", "natgeo") ) map(   style="satellite",   loadedTiles = list(     satellite = list(       maxZoom = 19     ),     lightgrey = list(),     natgeo = list(       maxZoom = 16     )   ) )

title: map-tiles
text: can use set_tile_layer() function update tiles map options defined loadedTiles map created.
code: library(shiny) library(toro)   ui <- fluidPage(   toro::mapOutput("map"),   selectInput(     "tiles",     "Tiles",     choices = toro::get_tile_options(),     selected = "satellite"   ), )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$map <- toro::renderMap({     toro::map(style = "satellite", loadedTiles = toro::get_tile_options())   })    observe({     req(input$tiles)     toro::mapProxy("map") |>       set_tile_layer(input$tiles)   }) }  if (interactive()) {   shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: map-tiles
text: can also change maps tiles via tile controller. tile controller must placed inside control panel. information adding controls map see controls vignette.
code: library(shiny) library(toro)  all_tiles <- toro::get_tile_options()  ui <- fluidPage(   toro::mapOutput("map") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$map <- toro::renderMap({     toro::map(style = "satellite", loadedTiles = all_tiles) |>       add_control_panel(         panel_id = "demo-panel"       ) |>       add_tile_selector_control(         available_tiles = all_tiles,         panel_id = "demo-panel"       )   })  }  if (interactive()) {   shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: popups

title: Integration with Shiny
text: modify map shiny page without re-rendering whole widget can use mapProxy. Typically use map create initial static map widget, use mapProxy update dynamic aspects map.
code: library(shiny) library(toro)   ui <- fluidPage(   toro::mapOutput("map"),   selectInput(     "tiles",     "Tiles",     choices = toro::get_tile_options(),     selected = "satellite"   ), )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$map <- toro::renderMap({     toro::map(style = "satellite", loadedTiles = toro::get_tile_options())   })    observe({     req(input$tiles)     toro::mapProxy("map") |>       set_tile_layer(input$tiles)   }) }  if (interactive()) {   shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Integration with Shiny
text: default map initially loads trigger loaded event Shiny. can use event trigger actions Shiny app. Shiny input value available input$<mapId>_loaded, <mapId> output ID map.
code: # In UI mapOutput("my_map")  # In server loaded events watching <map_id>_loaded input values observe({   req(input$my_map_loaded)   print("Map has loaded!") }) |>   bindEvent(input$my_map_loaded)

title: Integration with Shiny
text: Anytime feature (polygon, line, point) clicked map, feature_click event fired. can listen event using observer Shiny app. Just append _feature_click map ID get input ID (input$<mapId>_feature_click).
code: # In UI mapOutput("my_map")  # In server feature click events watching <map_id>_feature_click input values observe({   print(get_clicked_feature(input$my_map_feature_click)) })

title: Integration with Shiny
text: Whenever map view changes (zoom/bounds) bounds / zoom event fired. can listen event using observer Shiny app. Just append _bounds / _zoom map ID get input ID (input$<mapId>_bounds / input$<mapId>_zoom).
code: # In UI mapOutput("my_map")  # In server bounds / zoom events watching <map_id>_bounds / <map_id>_zoom input values observe({   print(input$my_map_bounds) })  observe({   print(input$my_map_zoom) })
